content:
The wood of the bench is hard, but it’s a familiar hardness. It’s the kind of support that doesn't lie to you. 

I sit here at Sheba’s, the scent of berbere and roasted coffee drifting from the kitchen, mingling with the damp, metallic smell of the Fillmore rain. I’ve seen the street outside go from a neon-lit jazz corridor to a ghost town of boarded windows, and now to this—a stretch of high-end
